summary: All 4 v1 findings discharged by commit 152d2a8e. F2's keystone fix — routing AssertionError.FormatValue through Json.FormatForDiagnostic → DiagnosticOutput — closes the JUnit Sensitive leak in one place and was consolidated across three prior FormatValue copies (OBP win). F1/F3 now verify file content, not input echo. F4 replaces RemoveAt with a synthesized string JsonPropertyInfo so non-string Sensitive keys stay visible while values render as '******'. 20/20 PLang tests + 9/9 filter unit tests pass. Committed junit_sensitive_masked.xml snapshot is visual proof.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: not re-assessed — no files in their prior pass were touched by 152d2a8e
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: v6 approved but missed F1 (input-echo assertion weakness) and F3 (no end-to-end mask test); v2 fix retroactively strengthens both
previous_reviews_assessed.security: finding #3 (opened on both results.json and junit.xml) was only partially closed at 9dc148f5; now fully closed by F2

dischargeVerification.evidence: TestReportWritesJunitXml.test.goal:10 asserts %report.content% contains '<testsuites'; TestReportIncludesCoverageTables.test.goal:10 asserts 'branchCoverage'. .pr files mapped to assert.contains correctly. Both pass in plang --test.

AssertionError.FormatValue routes through Json.FormatForDiagnostic → DiagnosticOutput. Committed snapshot junit_sensitive_masked.xml shows privateKey='******' inside <failure>. Unit test AssertionError_Message_MasksSensitiveViaDiagnosticOutput with LeakySecretRecord proves the Message does not leak.

New .test.goal pair TestReportMasksSensitiveVariables{,Junit} drives %MyIdentity% into AssertionError.Actual via sensitivefail.fixture.goal and asserts %report.content% contains '******' for both JSON and JUnit paths. Dropping DiagnosticOutput from report.cs:281 would break the tests.

SensitivePropertyFilter.Mask now synthesizes typeInfo.CreateJsonPropertyInfo(typeof(string), prop.Name) with Get=>'******' instead of RemoveAt. Unit test Sensitive_NonStringProperty_RendersMaskedValueNotStripped confirms byte[] renders as masked string with key name preserved.

crossCuttingObservations: OBP consolidation: three FormatValue copies merged into Json.FormatForDiagnostic — correct per 'behavior belongs to the owner'.
Subtle behavior change in DefaultAssertProvider (non-string values now JSON-serialize instead of ToString) does not break the suite; net improvement.
Build non-determinism: three rebuilt .pr files look clean; only no-op cleanups (dropped defaults) observed, no semantic drift.
